response:
To determine how many bottles Brittany needs, we first calculate the total amount of shampoo she uses in a leap year, which consists of 366 days. Since she washes her hair every other day, she uses shampoo on approximately 183 occasions, consuming 1/4 ounce each time, resulting in a total usage of 45.75 ounces. Given that each bottle contains 14 ounces, dividing the total usage by the bottle size reveals she needs roughly 3.27 bottles, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to ensure she has enough for the entire year at her [location].

With the calculation complete, Brittany should plan her shopping trip to the [store] well in advance to avoid running out of product before the year ends. By purchasing four 14-ounce bottles, she will have a total of 56 ounces, which provides a comfortable surplus of 10.25 ounces beyond her projected 45.75-ounce requirement. This extra amount ensures she can maintain her routine without interruption, even if she decides to wash her hair more frequently during the summer months or if there are any minor variations in her daily schedule.
